mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in industrial production (INDPRO) signals expanding manufacturing output and economic growth, which is positively correlated with corporate earnings and leads to positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact: historical positive correlation with SPX 1m returns
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year rank correlation between INDPRO MoM change and next 20-day S&P 500 return is ≤ 0.05, or the signal fails to beat a buy-and-hold strategy in at least 60% of 5-year periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the effective federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) exceeds 2.5%, the central bank is in a tightening stance, which increases discount rates and depresses equity valuations, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact: monetary tightening episodes are associated with negative short-term equity returns
failure_condition: Average 5-day return after FEDFUNDS crosses above 2.5% is not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.10), or the hit rate of negative returns is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: A concurrent rise in consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) and fall in unemployment (UNRATE) indicates an overheating economy that compels the central bank to tighten monetary policy, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The interaction is stronger than the sum of individual effects.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL MoM > 0.2% AND UNRATE MoM < -0.1 percentage point
evidence_refs: fact: Phillips curve and historical market reactions to stagflation
failure_condition: In out-of-sample periods where the trigger fires, the average 20-day S&P 500 return is non-negative, or the rank IC of the interaction signal is no better than the individual signals.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of high unemployment (UNRATE above 7%), an increase in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals strong labor market recovery, boosting consumer confidence and spending, which translates into positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact: payroll growth in high-unemployment regimes historically leads equity rallies
failure_condition: When UNRATE > 7% and PAYEMS increases, the subsequent 60-day S&P 500 return is negative in more than 40% of cases, or the average return is lower than the unconditional average in high-unemployment periods.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales (RSAFS) grow at an annualized rate exceeding 5% over a three-month period, it signals above-trend consumer spending that may lead to overheating and preemptive monetary tightening, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact: retail sales surges have preceded market pullbacks in 1999, 2006
failure_condition: Average 5-day return after trigger is not negative or the signal does not outperform the unconditional average in terms of Sharpe ratio over the test period.

mechanism_narrative: Joint positive monthly changes in industrial production (INDPRO) and housing starts (HOUST) indicate broad-based economic expansion across both manufacturing and construction, which supports corporate earnings growth and leads to positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-trading-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: Both INDPRO and HOUST increase month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact: simultaneous expansion in multiple sectors is a strong growth signal
failure_condition: The average 60-day S&P 500 return following joint positive changes is not significantly positive, or the signal fails to predict positive returns in at least 60% of occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: During a zero-lower-bound regime (FEDFUNDS ≤ 0.25%), an increase in FEDFUNDS above 0.25% signals the central bank's confidence in economic recovery and the beginning of policy normalization, which is interpreted positively by equity markets, resulting in positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact: rate hikes from near zero have historically been followed by short-term equity rallies (e.g., 2015)
failure_condition: The average 20-day return after exit from zero-lower-bound is not positive, or the hit rate of positive returns is less than 55%.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts (HOUST) fall below 800 thousand units, it signals a severely weak housing market, which depresses residential investment, consumer wealth, and overall economic growth, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact: housing starts below 800K associated with recessionary periods
failure_condition: Average 60-day return after HOUST falls below 800 is not negative, or the signal does not achieve a negative Sharpe ratio over the test window.
